entry.title: Self-Check Card 🧷 吾日三省吾身
entry.content:
Think and reflect, how to make my life better?

Life is hard, but from what I have learned so far, the journey to happiness is also the journey towards growth mindset. No matter what happens, always think about the following questions,

What have I learned?
How can I do better next time？

If I don’t blame, then each experience is a learning process rather a permanent failure label, right? This is the core of happy life.

Also remember this,

People are often unreasonable, illogical and self-centered. Forgive them anyway.

If you are kind, people may accuse you of selfish ulterior motives. Be kind anyway.

If you are successful, you will win some false friends and some true enemies. Succeed anyway.

If you are honest and frank, people may cheat you. Be honest and frank anyway.

What you spend years building, someone could destroy overnight. Build anyway.

If you find serenity and happiness, they maybe jealous. Be happy anyway.

The good you do today, people will often forget tomorrow. Do good anyway.

Give the world the best you have, and it may never be enough. Give the best you’ve got anyway.

You see, in the final analysis it is between you and God. It was never between you and them anyway.

entry.title: The Ultimate Secret From Sage
entry.content:
Cultivate myself, towards a better and more rational person… It’s a life-long persuit!

Think about the quote over and over again, do frequent retrospection and think about what’s the next step.

Just love it, I think this quote is “the ultimate secret” behind happiness.

entry.title: 6.824 Distributed Systems
entry.content: Case study is the core of this course, means many industrial level production distributed systems…

entry.title: Self-Check Card 🧷 Extensive Reading
entry.content:
Read… More.

Let’s read those fantastic book.

No.Book
1The Six Pillars of Self-Esteem
2Unequal Childhoods
3Mindset

entry.title: Self-Check Card 🧷 Systems Design
entry.content:
Master all the basic systems design principles, they are unescapable, and really helpful in your day to day work…

…The only thing I can do is to try to solve as many as problems, and analyze them thoroughly, case by case, and repetitively…

Let’s make this comprise of two parts, the first part is general system design, the second part will aim at machine learning system design.

Part I

No.Quiz
1Design Pastebin.com (or Bit.ly)
2Design the Twitter timeline and search (or Facebook feed and search)
3Design a web crawler
4Design Mint.com
5Design the data structures for a social network
6Design a key-value store for a search engine
7Design Amazon’s sales ranking by category feature
8Design a system that scales to millions of users on AWS

Part II

No.Quiz
1

entry.title: Self-Check Card 🧷 Algorithms
entry.content:
Master all the basic algorithms, they are unescapable in your CS study.

Problems those I can’t come up with solutions directly… Need to rethink again…!

No.Quiz
8String to Integer (atoi)
126Word Ladder II
168Excel Sheet Column Title
260Single Number III
300Longest Increasing Subsequence
384Shuffle an Array
424Longest Repeating Character Replacement
507Perfect Number
516Longest Palindromic Subsequence
525Contiguous Array
628Maximum Product of Three Numbers
738Monotone Increasing Digits
754Reach a Number
827Making A Large Island
834Sum of Distances in Tree
852Peak Index in a Mountain Array
877Stone Game
915Partition Array into Disjoint Intervals
1059All Paths from Source Lead to Destination
1155Number of Dice Rolls With Target Sum
1310XOR Queries of a Subarray
1316Distinct Echo Substrings
1372Longest ZigZag Path in a Binary Tree
1376Time Needed to Inform All Employees
1395Count Number of Teams
1539Kth Missing Positive Number
1605Find Valid Matrix Given Row and Column Sums
1638Count Substrings That Differ by One Character
1639Number of Ways to Form a Target String Given a Dictionary
1642Furthest Building You Can Reach
1700Number of Students Unable to Eat Lunch
1701Average Waiting Time
1740Find Distance in a Binary Tree
1792Maximum Average Pass Ratio
1796Second Largest Digit in a String
1805Number of Different Integers in a String
1806Minimum Number of Operations to Reinitialize a Permutation
1823Find the Winner of the Circular Game
1834Single-Threaded CPU
1839Longest Substring Of All Vowels in Order
1854Maximum Population Year

entry.title: 6.s081 Operating System Engineering
entry.content:
Being a craftsman… Only way to learn to do systems…

By following this course, I want to sum up the important concepts in operating systems field…

Theory Note, less is more!

entry.title: Complexity Analysis, Learning Report
entry.content:
I need note this, by writing the mathematical formulas by hands, I can retain them.

The best way to master these materials is through analyzing the real problems, and think them repetitively.

An illustration image of asymptotic notations involved in discussion.

Here, I need review the three notations in the asymptotic analysis of algorithms,

First, - notation

Definition:

Second, - notation

Definition:

Third, - notation

Definition:

Other notations in lectures,

Fourth, - notation

Definition:

Another popular way to illustrate this,

Fifth, - notation

Definition:

Another popular way to illustrate this,

Great way to draw an analogy between the asymptotic comparsion of two functions and and comparison of two real numbers and :
